blocks.content: Wednesday. Between second and third. The hallway was packed the way it always was — that two-minute window where everyone was somewhere and nobody was anywhere.
She came from the other direction. I saw her before she saw me. I had about four seconds to decide what to do with my face.
She saw me. And something happened — not a big thing. Just a shift. The way someone's face changes when they see a person they were already thinking about.
She didn't slow down. She was still walking. But she turned her shoulder toward me as she passed — and bumped it. Soft. Deliberate. Didn't look back.
I stood there for a second in the middle of the hallway while the crowd moved around me.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Friday can not come fast enough fr.

blocks.prompt: She bumped your shoulder on purpose. The crowd moved around you.

options.label: "She's just like that." You don't believe it either.
Don't answer. You're still standing in the middle of the hallway.
